item.aggregated_output:
---
name: hyperresearch-codex
description: |
  Run or continue the Codex Hyperresearch parity workflow. Use when the user asks for Hyperresearch inside Codex, Hyperresearch Codex parity, V8 research orchestration, fresh step loading, source provenance, critic/patch gates, or a durable start/advance Hyperresearch Codex run.
---

# Hyperresearch Codex

This skill is the Codex-facing operator manual for the Hyperresearch parity adapter.

## Current Runtime Boundary

- Reusable service and CLI implementation lives in `rawr-hq-template`.
- Current Codex skill, workflow, role-agent briefs, and hook/MCP adoption drafts live in downstream `rawr-hq` because that is the active agent-sync source.
- The current template implementation includes the synthetic slice plus a V8 start/advance state machine. Fixture light/full routes are the control-plane proof; live research quality still depends on actual Codex agent execution, agent-written artifacts, source capture, claim trace, and patch logs.

## Required First Move

After compaction, interruption, or a fresh session:

1. Read the service-package remainder plan when the template repo is available: `services/hyperresearch-codex/spec/REMAINDER_PLAN.md`.
2. Read the local references in this skill:
   - `references/remainder-plan.md`
   - `references/integration-spec.md`
   - `references/flows.md`
   - `references/parity-matrix.md`
   - `references/testing-plan.md`
   - `references/evidence.md`
   - `references/review-ledger.md`
   - `references/dra-runbook.md`
3. Compare current artifacts to the plan and update the changelog/decision log before continuing.

## V8 Control Protocol

After installing the template CLI topic, start a V8 run through RAWR:

```bash
bun run --cwd apps/cli rawr hyperresearch codex start \
  --query "<canonical research query>" \
  --vault "<vault-root>" \
  --steps "<step-reference-dir>" \
  --tier auto \
  --backend fixture \
  --json
```

Then loop:

```bash
bun run --cwd apps/cli rawr hyperresearch codex advance \
  --ledger "<vault-root>/research/temp/hyperresearch-codex-run.json" \
  --agent-mode packets \
  --backend fixture \
  --json
```

Use `--backend real` for a verified real Hyperresearch CLI proof. `validate` accepts the same backend flag for command-surface symmetry, although validation reads the ledger and does not execute backend CLI calls:

```bash
bun run --cwd apps/cli rawr hyperresearch codex validate \
  --ledger "<vault-root>/research/temp/hyperresearch-codex-run.json" \
  --backend real \
  --json
```

If `advance` returns `status: "awaiting_agents"`, read each returned packet path, spawn the named Codex custom agent, and require the agent to write the declared `expectedOutputPath`. Packet outputs must include declared `artifactWrites` for every assigned required artifact in that packet. Treat `artifactWrites` as artifact commitments: include newly written artifacts and carried-forward artifacts whose current content the agent verified. Each declaration must include the relative path, SHA-256 of the file content, and a short summary. The service fans in all packet outputs atomically and validates the full step artifact set before completion. Then run `advance` again. For fixture proof, use `--agent-mode synthesize` or the end-to-end fixture command:

```bash
bun run --cwd apps/cli rawr hyperresearch codex run-fixture \
  --query "<canonical research query>" \
  --vault "<vault-root>" \
  --steps "<step-reference-dir>" \
  --tier light \
  --json
```

Use `--backend real` only after the real Hyperresearch CLI command shapes and artifact expectations are verified for the specific run.

## Non-Negotiable Runtime Invariants

- Never use `hyperresearch research` as a parity substitute. It is a one-shot command, not the 16-step harness.
- Load every step reference fresh by path and hash before executing that step.
- Persist canonical query, tier route, current step, step loads, CLI calls, source captures, failures, resumes, and review dispositions in the run ledger.
- Treat `inspect` as read-only run state and `validate` as the integrity gate with `passed`, `blockingFindings`, and `warningFindings`.
- Use Hyperresearch CLI operations for vault/source/note/export behavior and record their stdout, stderr, and exit code.
- Treat hooks and MCP as optional guardrails until their feature flags/tool surfaces are proven in this environment.
- Treat post-synthesis edits as patch-only work with explicit patch logs and integrity gates.
- Completed V8 runs must include `research/claim-trace.json`; source URLs in the claim trace must map to captured source URLs or explicit uncertainty.
- Final report edits after the synthesis snapshot must have a covering `research/patch-log.json` entry with before/after hashes, rationale, and hunks.

## Agent Use

Use role-scoped agents for work that is parallelizable and has a clear output artifact. Each agent packet must include:

- canonical query,
- pipeline step,
- input artifact paths,
- required artifact paths,
- expected output path/schema,
- `artifactWrites` commitments for every assigned required artifact, each with `path`, `sha256`, and `summary`,
- `sourceUrls` for every URL that the service must capture through the Hyperresearch CLI; malformed source URLs block the run,
- provenance requirements,
- failure behavior.

The installed role briefs are projected as Codex custom agents under the active Codex agent home, usually `~/.codex-rawr/agents/hyperresearch-*.toml`. The parent coordinator remains accountable for fan-in, validation, and ledger updates.

</skill-usage-tracking>
<!-- Skill usage disclosure: On completion, state "Skills used: hyperresearch-codex" with optional rationale. Omit if no skills invoked. -->

item.aggregated_output: 394:- The bounded research program is intentionally closed as contained-lab coordination/proof, not production-runtime proof. The closeout path ran through PR `#271` for the final domino packet and PR `#272` for the program closeout itself. [Task 4]

# Higher-Order Runtime Proof Plan

This plan defines the first acceptance proof after the packet-provenance service contract. It is intentionally narrower than full Hyperresearch V8 parity: it proves live Codex/RAWR runtime behavior that was previously unproven, while keeping Hooks/MCP, full-tier depth work, and long-form research quality out of scope until separately validated.

## Baseline

- Template baseline: `220885d1 test(hyperresearch): prove codex rawr packet workflow`.
- Downstream baseline: `af264960 docs(hyperresearch): sync codex rawr proof evidence`.
- Completed claim: service implementation and packet provenance contract are green.
- Remaining target: runtime evidence that native `codex-rawr exec` can drive the synced Hyperresearch workflow with role-agent fan-out, resume, multiple source captures, final claim trace, and service validation.

## Proof Scope

Run a fresh `codex-rawr exec` session against the template worktree. The session must:

1. Use the installed `hyperresearch-codex` skill/workflow and the RAWR CLI surface:
   - `rawr hyperresearch codex start`
   - `rawr hyperresearch codex advance --agent-mode packets --backend real`
   - `rawr hyperresearch codex validate --backend real`
2. Start a light-tier real-backend V8 run for a bounded research query that can be supported by 2-4 public sources.
3. Stop at packet gates and use native Codex `spawn_agent`/wait/close behavior for the named Hyperresearch role agents.
4. Require each spawned role agent to read its packet and write the declared `expectedOutputPath` JSON.
5. Require packet outputs to include assigned `artifactWrites` with SHA-256 hashes and `sourceUrls` for parent-owned Hyperresearch CLI source capture.
6. Deliberately stop after at least one `awaiting_agents` gate and resume the same Codex session with `codex-rawr exec resume <session-id>`, using the same ledger.
7. Complete the route with at least 2 captured source URLs and at least 3 material final-report claims traced in `research/claim-trace.json`.
8. Preserve a service-local evidence subset under `services/hyperresearch-codex/spec/evidence/<date>-codex-rawr-runtime-proof/`.

## Query

Use this bounded query unless a blocker requires a same-sized replacement:

> What are the practical compatibility implications of Python's packaging metadata standards for modern Python project installers? Focus on how `pyproject.toml`, core metadata, and dependency groups affect installer behavior, and cite only Python Packaging Authority or official Python documentation sources.

Rationale:

- Public, stable, sourceable from official docs.
- Naturally supports 2-4 sources.
- Narrow enough for a light-route proof.
- Allows at least three factual claims without needing long-form full-tier depth work.

Preferred source URLs:

- `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/pyproject-toml/`
- `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/core-metadata/`
- `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/dependency-groups/`
- `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/tutorials/packaging-projects/`

## Acceptance Criteria

The proof is green only if all of these are true:

- `codex-rawr exec` event logs show at least one native `spawn_agent` call for a `hyperresearch-*` role agent during the real run, not only the separate capability probe.
- The run ledger records a resume event created by an `advance` call with a non-empty resume reason after `codex-rawr exec resume`.
- The ledger reaches `completed: true` and `validate` returns `passed: true`.
- The ledger records real backend CLI operations including `init`, `search`, at least two `fetch` calls, `note`, `lint`, `sync`, and `export`.
- `sourceCaptures` contains at least two distinct HTTP(S) URLs with CLI call indexes and suggested-by packet job provenance.
- `agentJobs` contains completed jobs for the light route packet roles.
- Every packet output has assigned `artifactWrites`; every declared hash matches the file content in the vault.
- The final report exists under `research/notes/`.
- `research/claim-trace.json` contains at least three claims whose text appears in the final report and whose source URLs map to captured source URLs.
- The checked-in evidence README explicitly states what this proof does not claim.

## Non-Claims

This proof does not claim:

- full-tier 16-step V8 research parity,
- Hooks/MCP runtime parity,
- long-form report quality parity,
- automatic parent-side orchestration without prompt guidance,
- global downstream plugin drift resolution,
- PR submission or release readiness.

## Evidence To Preserve

Preserve only reviewable evidence, not the full temp vault:

- wrapper prompt, final output, stderr, and JSONL events;
- start/advance/resume/validate command JSON output;
- final ledger;
- packet JSONs and packet result JSONs;
- final report;
- claim trace;
- captured source notes or exported vault JSON if present;
- a short proof README with session id, command surface, source URLs, role-agent evidence, resume evidence, validation status, and non-claims.

Do not preserve SQLite vault internals, browser/session secrets, auth material, or unrelated temp files.

## Review

After the proof, run a default-agent review pass focused on:

- whether the evidence actually proves native role-agent fan-out,
- whether resume was real and ledgered,
- whether claim trace/source capture validation is honest,
- whether specs and downstream material need updates,
- whether any topology or service-law regression was introduced.

- For this runtime-realization spec family, descriptor-table derivation must stay pre-provisioning and non-portable, and any runtime-bound values must flow through invocation context rather than closure capture. [Task 1]
- The current hotspot set was descriptor refs/table/registry, async step identity, schema import surface, `traceId` optionality, residual wording around “shared semantic ownership,” and cross-document stale canonical docs. [Task 1]
- The authority re-check confirmed the external authority snapshot was unchanged, while the repo-local runtime spec remained a different file with a different hash and shorter line count; repo-local docs are still a drift risk and should not be assumed authoritative. [Task 1]
- `tools/runtime-realization-type-env` is a contained spec-conformance lab, not production SDK/runtime code. `evidence/design-guardrails.md` defines proof semantics, and `evidence/runtime-spine-verification-diagnostic.md` is the derived R/Y/G status view rather than independent authority. [Task 2]
- The canonical continuity doc pair is `tools/runtime-realization-type-env/RUNBOOK.md` plus `tools/runtime-realization-type-env/evidence/phased-agent-verification-workflow.md`, with `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, `design-guardrails.md`, and `scripts/verify-structure.ts` pointing back to them. [Task 2]
- The lab’s documented verification entry point remains `bun run runtime-realization:type-env`, which maps to the full `runtime-realization-type-env:gate` sequence; the validated checks here were `bunx nx run runtime-realization-type-env:structural`, `bunx nx run runtime-realization-type-env:gate`, `bun run runtime-realization:type-env`, `git diff --check`, and `gt submit --stack --ai`. [Task 2]
- In this repo, `gt submit --stack --ai --publish --no-edit --no-interactive` is the working non-interactive way to publish the full runtime-realization stack, and `gt modify -a --no-interactive -m ...` is the amend path that preserves stack behavior. [Task 3]
- The phase-team/process rules now live in `evidence/runtime-realization-research-program.md`, `evidence/phased-agent-verification-workflow.md`, `evidence/workstreams/TEMPLATE.md`, and `evidence/workstreams/README.md`; PR `#259` is the coordination-program PR that carried those rules into review. [Task 3]
- The bounded research program is intentionally closed as contained-lab coordination/proof, not production-runtime proof. The closeout path ran through PR `#271` for the final domino packet and PR `#272` for the program closeout itself. [Task 4]
- The proof categories stay distinct at closeout: `proof`, `vendor-proof`, `simulation-proof`, `xfail`, `todo`, and `out-of-scope`. Before any promotion, re-check that the canonical runtime spec hash still matches `evidence/proof-manifest.json`. [Task 4]
- The residual-yellow inventory breaks into three classes: genuine design/policy decisions, deferred-but-obvious production engineering, and items fenced because the contained lab never claimed production authority. The likely next program is a production migration program with roughly five layers: decision compression, production host mounting, provider/config cut, observation/control-plane cut, and migration hardening. [Task 4]
- The durable startup packet for a fresh peer agent is the repo doc set, not the session transcript: `AGENTS.md`, `RUNBOOK.md`, the research program/workflow docs, current workstream report, `proof-manifest.json`, `runtime-spine-verification-diagnostic.md`, and the pinned canonical runtime spec. [Task 5]
- The recommended next workstream remained `ProviderEffectPlan -> Bootgraph/Provisioning Lowering` until the closeout reframed the program boundary; for any restart after closeout, the next agent should open an explicit residual decision packet or production migration workstream rather than resuming the bounded research loop. [Task 5]

After compaction, interruption, or a fresh session:

1. Read the service-package remainder plan when the template repo is available: `services/hyperresearch-codex/spec/REMAINDER_PLAN.md`.
2. Read the local references in this skill:
   - `references/remainder-plan.md`

## V8 Control Protocol

After installing the template CLI topic, start a V8 run through RAWR:

```bash

  --query "<canonical research query>" \
  --vault "<vault-root>" \
  --steps "<step-reference-dir>" \
  --tier auto \
  --backend fixture \
```

```bash
bun run --cwd apps/cli rawr hyperresearch codex advance \

```

```bash
bun run --cwd apps/cli rawr hyperresearch codex validate \
  --ledger "<vault-root>/research/temp/hyperresearch-codex-run.json" \
  --backend real \
  --json
```

If `advance` returns `status: "awaiting_agents"`, read each returned packet path, spawn the named Codex custom agent, and require the agent to write the declared `expectedOutputPath`. Packet outputs must include declared `artifactWrites` for every assigned required artifact in that packet. The service fans in all packet outputs atomically and validates the full step artifact set before completion. Then run `advance` again. For fixture proof, use `--agent-mode synthesize` or the end-to-end fixture command:

```bash
bun run --cwd apps/cli rawr hyperresearch codex run-fixture \
  --query "<canonical research query>" \
  --vault "<vault-root>" \
  --steps "<step-reference-dir>" \
  --tier light \
  --json
```

Use `--backend real` only after the real Hyperresearch CLI command shapes and artifact expectations are verified for the specific run.

## Non-Negotiable Runtime Invariants

- Never use `hyperresearch research` as a parity substitute. It is a one-shot command, not the 16-step harness.
- Load every step reference fresh by path and hash before executing that step.
- Persist canonical query, tier route, current step, step loads, CLI calls, source captures, failures, resumes, and review dispositions in the run ledger.
- Treat `inspect` as read-only run state and `validate` as the integrity gate with `passed`, `blockingFindings`, and `warningFindings`.
- Use Hyperresearch CLI operations for vault/source/note/export behavior and record their stdout, stderr, and exit code.
- Treat hooks and MCP as optional guardrails until their feature flags/tool surfaces are proven in this environment.
- Treat post-synthesis edits as patch-only work with explicit patch logs and integrity gates.
- Completed V8 runs must include `research/claim-trace.json`; source URLs in the claim trace must map to captured source URLs or explicit uncertainty.
- Final report edits after the synthesis snapshot must have a covering `research/patch-log.json` entry with before/after hashes, rationale, and hunks.

## Agent Use

Use role-scoped agents for work that is parallelizable and has a clear output artifact. Each agent packet must include:

- canonical query,
- pipeline step,
- input artifact paths,
- required artifact paths,
- expected output path/schema,
- `artifactWrites` commitments for every assigned required artifact, each with `path`, `sha256`, and `summary`,
- `sourceUrls` for every URL that the service must capture through the Hyperresearch CLI; malformed source URLs block the run,
- provenance requirements,
- failure behavior.

The installed role briefs are projected as Codex custom agents under the active Codex agent home, usually `~/.codex-rawr/agents/hyperresearch-*.toml`. The parent coordinator remains accountable for fan-in, validation, and ledger updates.

This plan defines the first acceptance proof after the packet-provenance service contract. It is intentionally narrower than full Hyperresearch V8 parity: it proves live Codex/RAWR runtime behavior that was previously unproven, while keeping Hooks/MCP, full-tier depth work, and long-form research quality out of scope until separately validated.

## Baseline

- Template baseline: `220885d1 test(hyperresearch): prove codex rawr packet workflow`.
- Downstream baseline: `af264960 docs(hyperresearch): sync codex rawr proof evidence`.
- Completed claim: service implementation and packet provenance contract are green.
- Remaining target: runtime evidence that native `codex-rawr exec` can drive the synced Hyperresearch workflow with role-agent fan-out, resume, multiple source captures, final claim trace, and service validation.

## Proof Scope

Run a fresh `codex-rawr exec` session against the template worktree. The session must:

1. Use the installed `hyperresearch-codex` skill/workflow and the RAWR CLI surface:
   - `rawr hyperresearch codex start`
   - `rawr hyperresearch codex advance --agent-mode packets --backend real`
   - `rawr hyperresearch codex validate --backend real`
2. Start a light-tier real-backend V8 run for a bounded research query that can be supported by 2-4 public sources.
3. Stop at packet gates and use native Codex `spawn_agent`/wait/close behavior for the named Hyperresearch role agents.
4. Require each spawned role agent to read its packet and write the declared `expectedOutputPath` JSON.
5. Require packet outputs to include assigned `artifactWrites` with SHA-256 hashes and `sourceUrls` for parent-owned Hyperresearch CLI source capture.
6. Deliberately stop after at least one `awaiting_agents` gate and resume the same Codex session with `codex-rawr exec resume <session-id>`, using the same ledger.
7. Complete the route with at least 2 captured source URLs and at least 3 material final-report claims traced in `research/claim-trace.json`.
8. Preserve a service-local evidence subset under `services/hyperresearch-codex/spec/evidence/<date>-codex-rawr-runtime-proof/`.

## Query

Use this bounded query unless a blocker requires a same-sized replacement:

> What are the practical compatibility implications of Python's packaging metadata standards for modern Python project installers? Focus on how `pyproject.toml`, core metadata, and dependency groups affect installer behavior, and cite only Python Packaging Authority or official Python documentation sources.

Rationale:

- Public, stable, sourceable from official docs.
- Naturally supports 2-4 sources.
- Narrow enough for a light-route proof.
- Allows at least three factual claims without needing long-form full-tier depth work.

Preferred source URLs:

- `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/pyproject-toml/`
- `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/core-metadata/`
- `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/dependency-groups/`
- `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/tutorials/packaging-projects/`

## Acceptance Criteria

The proof is green only if all of these are true:

- `codex-rawr exec` event logs show at least one native `spawn_agent` call for a `hyperresearch-*` role agent during the real run, not only the separate capability probe.
- The run ledger records a resume event created by an `advance` call with a non-empty resume reason after `codex-rawr exec resume`.
- The ledger reaches `completed: true` and `validate` returns `passed: true`.
- The ledger records real backend CLI operations including `init`, `search`, at least two `fetch` calls, `note`, `lint`, `sync`, and `export`.
- `sourceCaptures` contains at least two distinct HTTP(S) URLs with CLI call indexes and suggested-by packet job provenance.
- `agentJobs` contains completed jobs for the light route packet roles.
- Every packet output has assigned `artifactWrites`; every declared hash matches the file content in the vault.
- The final report exists under `research/notes/`.
- `research/claim-trace.json` contains at least three claims whose text appears in the final report and whose source URLs map to captured source URLs.
- The checked-in evidence README explicitly states what this proof does not claim.

## Non-Claims

This proof does not claim:

- full-tier 16-step V8 research parity,
- Hooks/MCP runtime parity,
- long-form report quality parity,
- automatic parent-side orchestration without prompt guidance,
- global downstream plugin drift resolution,
- PR submission or release readiness.

## Evidence To Preserve

Preserve only reviewable evidence, not the full temp vault:

- wrapper prompt, final output, stderr, and JSONL events;
- start/advance/resume/validate command JSON output;
- final ledger;
- packet JSONs and packet result JSONs;
- final report;
- claim trace;
- captured source notes or exported vault JSON if present;
- a short proof README with session id, command surface, source URLs, role-agent evidence, resume evidence, validation status, and non-claims.

Do not preserve SQLite vault internals, browser/session secrets, auth material, or unrelated temp files.

## Review

After the proof, run a default-agent review pass focused on:

- whether the evidence actually proves native role-agent fan-out,
- whether resume was real and ledgered,
- whether claim trace/source capture validation is honest,
- whether specs and downstream material need updates,
- whether any topology or service-law regression was introduced.
